title: Quantum Darwinism: Emergence of Classical Reality
description: Explore how classical objectivity emerges from quantum mechanics through environmental selection. Learn about redundant encoding, pointer states, and the quantum-to-classical transition through Zurek's quantum Darwinism framework.
learningObjectives: Explain the measurement problem and why classical objectivity requires explanation
Define pointer states and their role in environment-induced superselection
Calculate mutual information between system and environment fragments
Analyze redundancy in quantum information spreading to environment
Apply quantum Darwinism to understand the emergence of classical reality
Distinguish between quantum Darwinism and standard decoherence
